items.definition_en: A settlement is an official agreement to end a dispute.
items.example_en: The two companies finally reached a settlement.

items.definition_en: An obligation is something you must do because of a rule or agreement.
items.example_en: Each party has an obligation to follow the contract terms.

items.definition_en: A memorandum of understanding is an informal agreement between two parties.
items.example_en: They drafted a memorandum of understanding to outline their intentions.

items.definition_en: An endorsement is an official statement of support or approval.
items.example_en: The contract is valid only with the company's endorsement.

items.definition_en: A breach is a failure to follow the terms of an agreement.
items.example_en: A breach of contract can lead to legal action.

items.definition_en: A provision is a condition or term in a contract.
items.example_en: We need to add a confidentiality provision to the document.

items.definition_en: An addendum is a section added to a contract after it is signed.
items.example_en: They included an addendum to address new issues.

items.definition_en: A disclosure is the act of making new information known.
items.example_en: The agreement requires the disclosure of any conflicts of interest.

items.definition_en: A concession is something you agree to give up during an agreement.
items.example_en: She made a concession to help close the deal.

items.definition_en: Arbitration is a way to settle disputes without going to court.
items.example_en: The contract requires arbitration in case of disagreement.

items.definition_en: A counter-offer is a new offer made in response to another offer.
items.example_en: They rejected the first proposal but accepted the counter-offer.

items.definition_en: A covenant is a formal agreement or promise in a legal document.
items.example_en: There is a covenant requiring both sides to maintain secrecy.
